programme.desc: Exercise for seniors that can be done seated or standing.

programme.title: (Bumper + 3 Mid Show Breaks + End Break)
programme.desc: When you feel surrounded by pressure and fear, it's easy to lose perspective. But what if God's already provided the help you've been praying for? Keep it in context: the battle isn't proof you're losing, it's evidence that you're carrying something.

programme.title: Expecting Change
programme.desc: God doesn't just want to fix you, He wants to restore you. Right now, He's transforming you to impact others. Expect more than a personal breakthrough; expect God to exceed your expectations and bless others through your life.

programme.title: Faith to Stay
programme.desc: When we're going through a season that's uncomfortable or uncertain, we often look for a way out. But Jesus demonstrates another way that leads to lasting transformation and fulfillment if we have the faith to follow His example.

programme.title: Try Higher
programme.desc: Don't try harder. In Try Higher, Pastor Steven Furtick shows us that no matter what obstacle stands in our way, we can bring it to the One who has the power to overcome it.

programme.title: On My Way Back
programme.desc: When it feels like God's taking you the long way, remember: He's worthy of your wait. If you're feeling discouraged or struggling with obedience while you wait, start by being honest with God about where you are and trusting that His timing.

programme.title: He Was The One
programme.desc: Assume that God is using you. In He Was The One, Pastor Steven teaches us that instead of judging our current season, we should trust that it is significant to where God is taking us next.

programme.title: Now Turn North
programme.desc: God didn't intend for you to live in this struggle; He meant for you to pass through it. This sermon will encourage you to head in the direction of your future and not turn back.

programme.title: Navigating Not Enough
programme.desc: I am enough because He is enough. In Navigating Not Enough, Pastor Steven Furtick reveals that if God put you in it, then He's given you what you need.

programme.title: The Blessing Of Letting Go
programme.desc: In The Blessing Of Letting Go, Pastor Steven Furtick teaches us that when we worship God in the middle of our situation, it relieves the pressure we feel when we try to handle it on our own.

programme.desc: Ken (Ken Jeong) and Julie (Kate Simses) disagree over allowing a critically ill stand-up comic (guest star George Wyner) to perform at a benefit show. Allison (Suzy Nakamura) has a crisis of confidence when she learns that her former patient is seeing a new therapist.

programme.title: Ken's An Expert Witness
programme.desc: Ken (Ken Jeong) serves as an expert witness in a courtroom appearance that leaves many doubting his medical expertise. Dave (Albert Tsai) sews a prom dress for Molly (Krista Marie Yu).

programme.title: Dave's Sex Talk
programme.desc: When Ken (Ken Jeong) avoids answering his questions about sex, Dave (Albert Tsai) turns to Julie (Kate Simses) for answers. Molly (Krista Marie Yu) gives Allison (Suzy Nakamura) a wardrobe makeover.

programme.title: Korean Men's Club
programme.desc: To keep Ken (Ken Jeong) from meddling in her book club, Allison (Suzy Nakamura) convinces him to join a civic-minded Korean Men's Club. Molly (Krista Marie Yu) asks Dave (Albert Tsai) for advice on attracting a nerdy classmate.

programme.title: Ken Tries Standup
programme.desc: Seeing a college classmate at the Laugh Factory inspires Ken (Ken Jeong) to pursue his dream of being a stand-up comic. Dave (Albert Tsai) enlists Molly (Krista Marie Yu) to train him for a push-up contest with their grandfather, D.K. (guest star Dana Lee).

programme.title: Allison's Career Move
programme.desc: Ken tries to keep Allison from accepting a job offer at the clinic – until he learns she doesn't want it anyway. As Dave conspires to keep their mom and dad from seeing Molly's SAT scores, Clark admits that he doesn't know what his boyfriend does for a living.

programme.title: Ken And Allison Share A Patient
programme.desc: It's Allison's first day of work at Welltopia and she and Ken try to prove the other wrong when it comes to a patient's diagnosis.

programme.title: Dr. Ken's Banquet Snub
programme.desc: Ken is surprised to learn that Pat is replacing him as emcee of the HMO's annual banquet. Clark worries about how much Damona argues with her boyfriend, while Molly discovers that Dave has a stalker.

programme.title: Dr. Ken: Child Of Divorce
programme.desc: When an alarmed Ken finds out D.K. is divorced, he thinks it means his own marriage is doomed to failure.

programme.title: D.K.'S Korean Ghost Story
programme.desc: When Allison worries that Dave's rejection of Halloween means he’s growing up too fast, D.K. looks to bring them together with an ancient Korean ghost story.

programme.title: Ken Learns Korean
programme.desc: After a card game with his dad's friends exposes his inability to speak Korean, Ken (Ken Jeong) joins Dave (Albert Tsai) at his language class. Clark (Jonathan Slavin) and Pat (Dave Foley) square off over a new nurses' contract.

programme.title: De-Void
programme.desc: With the Nogitsune still in control of their friend Stiles, Scott and Lydia find an unlikely ally in Peter Hale. Elsewhere, the effects of the Nogitsune have given Chris, Alison and Kira some trouble with four out-of-control werewolves.

programme.title: Insatiable
programme.desc: Scott (Tyler Posey) and company race to rescue Lydia (Holland Roden) from the clutches of evil and get help from a young woman who has escaped from the metal hospital. But the Nogitsune makes a surprise move, resulting in a tragic loss to the group.

programme.title: The Divine Move
programme.desc: In the Season 4 finale, struck by disaster and nearly defeated, Scott (Tyler Posey) and Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) call on their friends and allies to make one last stand against the Nogitsune. And someone thought dead makes a surprising return.

programme.title: The Dark Moon
programme.desc: Scott and his Pack journey into Mexico to rescue a missing friend.
Scott must turn to an unlikely ally in order to stop Kate's plan.

programme.title: Muted
programme.desc: A new Freshman player threatens Scott's position on the Lacrosse team. Stilinski investigates a brutal murder.

programme.title: The Benefactor
programme.desc: Uninvited guests arrive at Lydia's lake house on the night of the full moon. Derek tracks down a killer.

programme.title: I.E.D.
programme.desc: As another name is scratched off the list, Lydia is required to find the second key, thus revealing more targets. Scott, Stiles and Kira are on high alert after realizing that the assassin is on the lacrosse team.

programme.title: Tempests
programme.desc: Attempting to deliver serum to a plague-stricken planet, a pilot (Eric McCormack) and his crew crash land on an unknown moon. Bitten by an alien spider while fixing the ship, the pilot's mind wanders between alternate realities…in The Outer Limits.

programme.title: The Awakening
programme.desc: A woman unable to feel emotions gets a new lease on life when a brain implant allows her to experience feelings for the first time. But, when the implant malfunctions, driving her towards insanity, she is shocked at the real cause of her problems.

programme.title: New Lease
programme.desc: A remorseless, uncaring scientist (Stephen Lang) and his partner (Michael Ontkean) discover a way to bring life back to the recently deceased. But after the scientist is killed and brought back to life himself, will he choose to live differently?

programme.title: Double Helix
programme.desc: Brilliant geneticist Martin Nodel (Ron Rifkin) unlocks a secret code imprinted on human DNA. But when he tests this code on himself, his "instincts" draw him to a remote location where he finds a trip…to The Outer Limits. Ryan Reynolds co-stars.

programme.title: Dead Man's Switch
programme.desc: When it is not known if aliens headed to Earth are friend or foe, five people are placed in secret bunkers to man switches that, if not pressed at specified intervals, will set off mass destruction that will destroy everything, alien or human alike.

programme.title: Hearts And Minds
programme.desc: Engaged as soldiers on a distant planet to prevent a race of alien bugs from stealing minerals that provide the Earth's energy, a woman (Christine Elise) and her troops accidentally discover that the real enemy may not be the aliens at all.

programme.title: Music Of The Spheres
programme.desc: When a seemingly harmless audio signal from space causes physiological changes in Earth's youth, a young scientist races against time to discover what exactly is happening and why. Could the changes be necessary for the survival of the planet?

programme.title: In Another Life
programme.desc: A despondent man finds himself torn between killing himself and killing his co-workers. Before he can do either, he's pulled into The Outer Limits where there are hundreds of versions of him, each with a different life and a different character.

programme.title: The Revelations Of 'Becka Paulson
programme.desc: A woman's life changes dramatically when she accidentally shoots herself in the head which causes her to not only grow more intelligent, but also to realize how dull her life was before the photo in a picture frame started talking to her.

programme.title: Bodies Of Evidence
programme.desc: When an incident at a space station leaves three people dead, the captain of the ship returns to Earth to face murder charges. Is the captain a murderous madman? Or has he actually seen the face of evil floating in the zero-gravity of deep space?

programme.title: Shadow Valley
programme.desc: The crew ventures into a dark valley across from camp, searching for signs of anything elusive..

programme.title: Kimball Gets Fired
programme.desc: Ralph (Mary Grace Canfield) is distraught when her beloved Mr. Kimball, is fired as the county agricultural agent. Out of a job, Kimball (Alvy Moore) no longer has money enough to marry the lady-carpenter-in-waiting! But wait�did he even want to?

programme.title: The Vulgar Ring Story
programme.desc: Lisa weaves the tale of her great, great grandmother, the Queen of the Gypsies, to explain a bizarre family tradition: a woman in her family must marry an American -- but only every fourth generation -- in order to gain a special inheritance.

programme.title: Who's Lisa
programme.desc: After Oliver (Eddie Albert) accidentally drops a hammer on her head, Lisa (Eva Gabor) develops a case amnesia, with no idea who she is or why she is on a farm in Hooterville�and she adamantly refuses to believe that Oliver is her husband!

programme.title: Music To Milk By
programme.desc: Nothing gets done on the Douglas farm when Oliver (Eddie Albert) gifts Eb (Tom Lester) a top-of-the-line radio for his birthday. Letting nothing stop him, Eb (Tom Lester) enters a radio contest...even when Eleanor the cow swallows the radio whole.

programme.title: The Man For The Job
programme.desc: It's a very official third season premiere for Green Acres when the retirement of a state senator leaves Hooterville considering its best candidate for the job. Of course, they turn to their beloved lawyer-come-farmer Oliver Douglas (Eddie Albert).

programme.title: Lisa's Jam Session
programme.desc: Oliver finds himself in a jam after asking Lisa to make him homemade fruit preserves. An episode deliciously devoted to Lisa's culinary escapades, Lisa musters all of her Hungarian might to make Oliver the best peach jam he's ever tasted.

programme.title: Oliver Vs The Phone Company
programme.desc: After years of substandard service, Oliver is hellbent on throwing the book at the Hooterville Phone Company. When it becomes clear that the local utility oversight board can't do much about it, he decides to handle matters directly.

programme.title: Oliver Takes Over The Phone Company
programme.desc: Mr. Trendell, the president of the Hooterville Phone Company, hands the company over to a delighted Oliver. But after a day of crossed wires and a switchboard repairman with some lose connections of his own, Oliver is ready to call it quits.

programme.title: Don't Count Your Tomatoes Before They're Picked
programme.desc: For the first time, Oliver has a successful crop that needs picking. His tomatoes have grown beautifully. But matters at the phone company continue divide his precious time, so Oliver looks for an interested buyer to take the company off his hands.

programme.title: A Kind Word For The President
programme.desc: Being president of the Hooterville Phone Company has been a rude awakening for Oliver. The switchboard operator is now calling him at five in the morning! After a bevy of new complaints from unhappy customers, Oliver has just about had enough.

programme.title: Eb Elopes
programme.desc: One might think that when Eb takes a vacation things around the farm might go more smoothly. But when Eb elopes, leaving his cousin Walter to take his place, it turns out to be a horrible idea. Walter barely knows a weed from a hole in the ground!

programme.title: The Thing
programme.desc: Lisa is reminded that she stored a birthday present for Oliver years ago: a genuine Stavinsky. But what is it? It's a modern art sculpture that is two parts plumbing pipes, one part tire chains, and one part birdbath. Oliver calls it "The Thing."

programme.title: Das Lumpen
programme.desc: The continually evolving story of how Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Oliver) fell in love gets yet another version in this delightful episode. But one thing is for sure: Lisa and Oliver are just as in love today as the day when he fell out of the sky.

programme.title: Jealousy, English Style
programme.desc: Green Acres is greener than ever, with jealousy. When Oliver (Eddie Albert) hires a handsome, young, intelligent Brit to take over his farm work while he is away, it doesn't take long for the newcomer to make a strong impression on Lisa (Eva Gabor).

programme.title: Haney's New Image
programme.desc: Mr. Haney is up to his money-making schemes again. When a government representative tells Mr. Haney that the state is looking to purchase the Douglas farm for a new highway, Mr. Haney arranges to buy back the farm from the Douglases at a steal.

programme.title: Alf And Ralph Break Up
programme.desc: Ralph, the lady carpenter, runs away from home and stays with the Douglases after having a fight with her brother, Alf. While Oliver may never get his bedroom finished, he gains a maid as Ralph takes over as the Douglases' live-in housekeeper.

programme.title: No Trespassing
programme.desc: Picking apples while on a picnic, Oliver runs into the feisty Ira Hatch, the owner of the apple orchard. However, once back in town, Mr. Drucker tells Oliver that Hatch has been dead for years. An incredulous Oliver agrees to see a psychologist.

programme.title: Lurch Learns To Dance
programme.desc: Lurch becomes a veritable gazelle when he takes dancing lessons at Gomez and Morticia's suggestion, after he admits to being a wallflower at social functions. Each member of the Addams Family teaches Lurch a different step.

programme.title: Art And The Addams Family
programme.desc: Grandmama's paintings leave an art critic speechless, so Gomez sends for his friend, Sam Picasso, to teach Grandmama to paint in the abstract. The starving artist ignores Grandmama's and Morticia's work as he eats and paints.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets A Beatnik
programme.desc: Rockland Cartright III is a beatnik who crashes his motorbike into a tree and recuperates at the Addams house. Each regards the other as an oddity, although the family learns to appreciate certain aspects of Rockland's life.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The Undercover Man
programme.desc: Mr. Briggs, the postman, is hired by the government to do undercover work in the Addams house. While pretending to be a stamp collector, he investigates rumors that coded radio messages are being sent from the house.

programme.title: Mother Lurch Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Lurch's tiny fire-eating mother visits and demands that Lurch's employers wait on her boy hand-and-foot after Lurch leads her to believe that it's his house. Lurch revels in the unexpected attention he gets from Morticia and Gomez.

programme.title: Uncle Fester's Illness
programme.desc: Uncle Fester's electrical output fails abruptly. Should they call a doctor or an electrician to bring Fester back? Morticia prescribes a bowl of porcupine mold, and both she an Gomez agree that long exposure to moon rays would be beneficial.

programme.title: The Addams Family Splurges
programme.desc: The family would like to vacation on the moon, but the billion dollar travel cost is slightly prohibitive. Uncle Fester volunteers $21.13 and Morticia, using Gomez's new supercomputer, suggests placing a $2,000 bet on the daily double horse race.

programme.title: Cousin Itt Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Cousin Itt is advocated as curator of the local zoo. Gomez uses his influence with Park Commissioner Fisk (guest star Alan Reed) to promote Itt for the job, but the zookeepers are convinced the Addamses' cousin should be confined to a cage.

programme.title: The Addams Family In Court
programme.desc: Grandmama (Blossom Rock) is hauled off to jail for setting up a fortune-telling booth in the Addamses' living room. Gomez (John Astin) volunteers to act as attorney, and the court session becomes a shambles.

programme.title: Amnesia In The Addams Family
programme.desc: Gomez (John Astin) loses his memory after hitting his head; he fails to recognize Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and says the house looks like a condemned museum. The Addamses take turns hitting Gomez on the head trying to help him regain his memory.

programme.title: Thing Is Missing
programme.desc: Thing disappears from his box. Failing to find a single trace of Thing, Gomez (John Astin) conducts an investigation--with all members of the household under suspicion. Also starring Carolyn Jones, Jackie Coogan, and Ted Cassidy.

programme.title: Crisis In The Addams Family
programme.desc: Uncle Fester applies for a job as an insurance salesman with the same company which is threatening to cancel the Addamses' policy (because Fester keeps blowing things up). Fester convinces the company interviewer that he is qualified for the job.

programme.title: Lurch And His Harpsicord
programme.desc: Lurch becomes inconsolable when Gomez donates the family antique harpsichord to a collector. Gomez and Morticia offer Lurch a balalaika and a set of drums. When this proves unsatisfactory, Gomez and Fester decide to build a new harpsichord.

programme.title: Morticia, The Breadwinner
programme.desc: When Morticia hears the stock market has crashed, she assumes that Gomez has been financially wiped out and rallies the family to help make money. Morticia gives lessons, Lurch and Fester run an escort service, and the kids run a poison drink stand.

programme.title: The Addams Family And The Spaceman
programme.desc: While enjoying a moonlight picnic and snail hunt, the Addams Family is mistaken for Martians by uniformed investigators from Mysterious Space Objects Headquarters. In turn, Gomez (John Astin) and the clan regard their inquisitors with suspicion.

programme.title: My Son, The Chimp
programme.desc: An organ grinder's fugitive monkey leads Fester to believe that he has turned Pugsley into a monkey. Morticia and Gomez treat the monkey as though he were their son, welcoming him to the dinner table and giving him Pugsley's chores.

programme.title: Morticia's Favorite Charity
programme.desc: Moriticia's favorite charity is having an auction--so Morticia persuades her family to part with things to sell. Donations include Wednesday's headless doll, the old flogging table, a stuffed vulture and Uncle Fester's dynamite.

programme.title: MeatEater - First Timers: Montana Mule Deer Part 1
programme.desc: Television personality Joe Rogan and his best friend, comedian Bryan Callen, join Steven Rinella for their very first hunt. On a float trip for mule deer through the rugged Missouri Breaks, this is as adventurous of a first hunt as anyone could hope for, and Steve's special guests are in for a memorable experience and delicious wild game field cooking in some gorgeous country.

programme.title: MeatEater - First Timers: Montana Mule Deer Part 2
programme.desc: Television personality Joe Rogan and his best friend, comedian Bryan Callen, join Steven Rinella for their very first hunt. On a float trip for mule deer through the rugged Missouri Breaks, this is as adventurous of a first hunt as anyone could hope for, and Steve's special guests are in for a memorable experience and delicious wild game field cooking in some gorgeous country.

programme.title: MeatEater - Swamp Stew: Michigan Bowfishing
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to his cherished boyhood stomping grounds along Michigans Muskegon River.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Wide Open: New Mexico Antelope
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and friend Cody Lujan head to Northeastern New Mexico to hunt pronghorn antelope, the fastest big game animal in the United States. In the wide open desert plains, Steve will have to work hard to stalk within range of a mature buck. The payoff will be antelope loin topped with local New Mexico chiles.

programme.title: MeatEater - Primal Cuts: Bone-in Cooking Special
programme.desc: In this cooking special, Steven Rinella shows his fans some of his favorite preparations for big, bone-in cuts of red meat. Interspersed with highlights from his recent caribou, buffalo, and mule deer hunts, the show features recipes that utilize some of the most underappreciated parts of big game animals. This episode is an attractive and delicious tribute to Steve's gun-to-table ethic.

programme.title: MeatEater - El Venado Blanco: Mexico Coues Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to his favorite hunting grounds of Sonora, Mexico to hunt for a Coues deer buck during the peak rutting season. Steve sets up camp and spends days glassing mountains and canyons for these elusive, wary desert whitetails. Meanwhile, his hunting partner, Remi Warren, attempts the difficult task of stalking one with a bow. To cap off the hunt, Steve cooks a batch of venison ribs braised in a Dutch oven buried beneath hot coals.

programme.title: MeatEater - Clash of the Titans: Northern California Pig & Deer
programme.desc: In this episode, Steven Rinella joins forces with fellow hunter and wild game chef Hank Shaw in the hills of Central California to go after Columbia blacktail deer, wild pigs, and a handful of small game species that are in season. The end result is a culinary smorgasbord of epic proportions.

programme.title: MeatEater - Sea Bear: Alaska Spring Bear and Seafood
programme.desc: Steve ventures out to his hunting and fishing shack on a remote coastline of southeast Alaska's Prince of Wales Island. While hunting bears from a skiff and a canoe, Steve gathers a variety of prime seafood and makes a discovery about his own motivations as a bear hunter.

programme.title: MeatEater - Full Boar: Whole Hog Cooking Special
programme.desc: Steven Rinella breaks down an entire boar and shares some of his favorite recipes in this Cooking Special. Matt Weingarter helps steve make head cheese, pork skin cracklins, smoked pork butt, and a tied-roast boned-out ham. Each dish requires thoughtful butchering, processing, and cooking to execute to perfection.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bull by Boat: British Columbia Moose
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan float a remote river on a bull moose hunt.
